meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve no portfolio (Advanced Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome no portfolio. A Advanced-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS Starter Kit to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Overcome the 'no portfolio' trap by building high-value SaaS products. Learn to use Antigravity and Claude Code to ship professional apps at lightning speed.
content.answer_block: The 'no portfolio' trap is a bottleneck to high-ticket revenue. By deploying a modular SaaS starter kit, you create a repeatable technical foundation. Use Antigravity to scaffold architecture and Claude Code to automate implementation, allowing you to showcase production-grade SaaS features to clients in hours, not weeks.
content.intro:
The blank screen of a portfolio is the silent killer of a freelance career. You have the skills, but without a concrete artifact to show a prospective client, you are just another voice in the noise. The industry doesn't reward potential; it rewards proof of work, and the fastest way to bridge that gap is to stop 'building projects' and start deploying products.

Today, we shift your workflow. We are moving away from manual coding and into the era of AI-assisted engineering. By leveraging a battle-tested SaaS starter kit, you transform your development process into an assembly line. This isn't just about code—it's about building a scalable engine that proves your technical authority to every client you pitch.

steps.title: Architecting the Core Stack
steps.description: Define the foundational requirements for your SaaS starter kit—authentication, database schema, and payment integration. Speed is the priority here.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a robust SaaS architecture using Next.js 14, Supabase for backend, and Stripe for billing. Include role-based access control and a clean, responsive layout.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'initialize project with Next.js, Supabase, and Stripe boilerplate'

steps.title: Automating Identity Management
steps.description: Implement authentication early to ensure your portfolio piece feels like a real product. Users need to see sign-up and dashboard flows.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Implement secure user authentication using Supabase Auth. Include magic links, social login buttons, and protected route middleware.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'implement Supabase Auth and configure middleware for protected dashboard routes'

steps.title: Integrating Payment Logic
steps.description: A SaaS without payments is just a hobby app. Integrate Stripe to demonstrate your capability to handle real-world transactions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Stripe subscription workflow. Include a pricing table component and webhook handlers for successful checkout events.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'set up stripe integration with webhooks and subscription plans'

steps.title: Building the 'Impress' Feature
steps.description: Add a high-impact AI feature—like an LLM-powered summary tool—to show you can integrate modern AI APIs, which is currently the highest-demand skill.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create an AI-powered text analysis feature using the OpenAI API. Implement a clean UI for input and streaming output.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'add ai feature with openai streaming support and clean ui'

steps.title: Deployment and Portfolio Packaging
steps.description: Deploy to Vercel and optimize for performance. A fast, live link is the ultimate proof of expertise for any client.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Optimize the project for Vercel deployment, generate a sitemap, and add meta-tags for SEO.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'optimize build, add seo tags, and prepare for production deployment'

pro_tips: Always build your SaaS starter kit as a monorepo to allow for rapid service expansion later.
Use mock data factories to populate your dashboard so it looks 'lived-in' and professional during demos.
Prioritize 'Time to First Render' in your UI; clients subconsciously judge your backend skill by frontend speed.
Wrap your AI features in a custom logging layer to monitor token usage—this shows clients you understand operational cost.
Document your technical decisions in a README file; it acts as a 'case study' that clients can read to understand your logic.
content.next_steps: Once your starter kit is live, create a 60-second Loom video walking through the dashboard and the code structure, then pin it to your portfolio.
content.conversion_block: Stop waiting for the 'perfect' client project. Build the engine you need to win the clients you want. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
